commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=fdcb36b2ba472e1bbfc4fb8ddd353c0858d10f79)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds exact cron watchdog timeout classification, suppresses matching failure-notification state and gateway failure notifications, and adds cron/gateway regression tests.

PR surface: Source +33, Tests +165. Total +198 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: configure an isolated cron job with a webhook or announce failure destination and return the exact tool-execution-started timeout; the PR return skips destination resolution. I did not run a live Matrix/cron setup in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human/contributor follow-up is needed because the branch still changes configured failure-destination delivery and lacks real setup proof.

target.title: fix(matrix): suppress noisy cron tool-timeout alerts
target.body:
## Summary
- suppress user-facing cron failure notifications for the exact isolated-runner watchdog timeout `cron: job execution timed out (last phase: tool-execution-started)`
- preserve the cron run as an error with `lastError` intact
- mark suppressed failure-notification delivery as `not-requested` instead of leaving it ambiguous
- keep ordinary cron failures, generic timeouts, webhook destinations, and provider/model failures notifying normally

## Proof
- Non-PII behavior proof uses synthetic cron jobs and the synthetic timeout reason `cron: job execution timed out (last phase: tool-execution-started)`; no real room IDs, user IDs, cron names, message IDs, or transcript content are included.
- Gateway behavior proof: `src/gateway/server.cron.test.ts` exercises both primary Matrix-style announce delivery and explicit failure-destination delivery. The synthetic tool-execution-started watchdog timeout records the cron error but does not send the user-facing failure notification.
- Focused gateway proof command: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server.cron.test.ts -t "does not announce tool-execution-started cron watchdog timeouts|does not send failure-destination notifications for tool-execution-started cron watchdog timeouts"` — 2 passed, 18 skipped.
- Cron state proof: `src/cron/service.persists-delivered-status.test.ts` verifies the timeout remains recorded while failure notification delivery is `not-requested`, including delivered and not-delivered result cases.
- Focused cron state proof command: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.cron.config.ts src/cron/service.persists-delivered-status.test.ts -t "keeps tool-execution-started timeouts recorded without requesting failure notification delivery|keeps primary announce tool-execution-started timeouts from expecting fallback failure notifications|suppresses failure notification state for delivered tool-execution-started timeouts|suppresses failure notification state for not-delivered tool-execution-started timeouts"` — 4 passed, 12 skipped.
- Full branch proof previously run and still reflected by the PR body: gateway cron tests 20 passed, cron delivery-status tests 16 passed, `git diff --check`, `pnpm -s lint:core`, and `pnpm -s tsgo:core:test`.

## Why no live transcript
A live Matrix room transcript or real cron artifact would expose private operational metadata. The proof therefore uses synthetic cron inputs and the exact public-safe watchdog timeout string to prove the user-facing suppression behavior without publishing private data.

## Notes
This intentionally does not hide the cron error from run state. It only suppresses the noisy Matrix/user-facing failure alert for the narrow false-alarm timeout where the embedded agent was already in tool execution.
target.author: bdjben
